Problem Statement: Find the limit of the following vector-valued functions at the indicated value of t .
Question: $$\underset{t \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \text{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ for $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 2 e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} \mathbf{\text{i}} + e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} \mathbf{\text{j}} + \text{ln} ( t - 1 ) \mathbf{\text{k}}$$
Answer: The limit does not exist because the limit of $$\text{ln} ( t - 1 )$$ as t approaches infinity does not exist.

1.  lim_{t→∞} 2e^{-t} = 2 · 0 = 0
2.  lim_{t→∞} e^{-t}  = 0
3.  lim_{t→∞} ln(t-1) = ∞     (the natural logarithm grows without bound)

Hence

lim_{t→∞} r(t) = 0 \mathbf i + 0 \mathbf j + ∞ \mathbf k.

Because the third component is unbounded, the vector does not approach a finite vector; it “shoots off” to infinity in the positive k–direction.  
Therefore the limit, in the usual finite sense, does not exist.
